summary: Auditable attribution repair record for scrutiny-blocking execution-state features whose implementation behavior is already present on HEAD.

repairs.scrutinyIssue: Recorded commit 63c32c1 was contaminated with unrelated files.

repairedAttribution.reason: Mission progress log records that contaminated commit 63c32c1 was replaced by isolated commit 321b693 containing only batch session-propagation files.

repairs.scrutinyIssue: Recorded commit c7b2a4e was unrelated E2E preflight script.

repairedAttribution.reason: Feature completed as explicit verification-only closure after prerequisite runtime fixes landed in earlier scoped commits; no single new code change was required in the assigned session.
evidence: task-dispatch, dispatch-coordinator, and worktree-commit contract validators recorded in the handoff

repairs.scrutinyIssue: Recorded commit 7c85a43 belonged to a different fail-closed completion feature.

repairedAttribution.reason: The assigned session was verification-only because the timeout regression had already been removed on HEAD before that worker ran. Follow-up tracing narrowed the smoke-timeout behavior to the smallest auditable implementation trail on the exercised code path: commit `504c261` introduced the shared dispatch coordinator and moved worktree-create onto that path, `7c9d267` tightened production-facing transition semantics used during dispatch preparation, and the later feature-scoped repair commits `0f9d9a0`, `168ce46`, and `97ab903` explicitly replaced the rejected broad-commit attribution with this narrower evidence trail. Together those scoped runtime and attribution-repair commits provide the auditable smoke-timeout record without relying on the unrelated broad lane reorganization in `0c72ab7` or the unrelated foundation refactor in `20a6a1b`.
evidence: progress_log.jsonl dismissal at 2026-03-11T05:30:27.753Z recording the original smoke-timeout blocker from resume-and-completion-contracts
integration-smoke validator output recorded in the handoff (`warcraft_worktree_create returns task spec with proper structure` passed in 6341.14ms)

justification.whyNoSingleScopedBehaviorCommit: The worker session that owned `worktree-create-smoke-timeout-fix` made no edits because HEAD already passed the targeted smoke path, so the repair must point to the narrower earlier runtime commits that changed the dispatch/spec-generation flow exercised by that smoke test rather than inventing a new code-only commit after the fact.
narrowingTrail: 7c85a43 was rejected because it only changed worktree completion fail-closed behavior, not smoke timeout handling
0c72ab7 was rejected because it touched 14 E2E files as a lane reorganization rather than a feature-scoped smoke-timeout repair

File-history tracing for `integration-smoke.test.ts`, `task-dispatch.test.ts`, `dispatch-coordinator.ts`, and `worktree-tools.test.ts` narrows the runtime changes relevant to the smoke path to commits 504c261 and 7c9d267 rather than the broader lane-reorg commit

Feature-scoped attribution-repair commits 0f9d9a0, 168ce46, and 97ab903 then record and tighten that narrowed evidence trail so follow-up scrutiny can audit the smoke-timeout fix without relying on either rejected broad commit
Commit 20a6a1b was removed from the attribution trail because it is a broad foundation refactor spanning many unrelated files rather than a feature-scoped smoke-timeout artifact
